text:
Once upon a gentle autumn in a small forest bordering a serene lake, a young owl named Aetharwoo lived in a cozy hollow near the water's edge. Every evening, Aetharwoo would scan the twilight sky for his favorite prey — fireflies.

One evening, as he perched on an old oak branch counting the stars, a glimmering glow caught his eye. It wasn't a firefly. It was a bat emitting a warm light, not common at all in the nocturnal forest.

Curious, Aetharwoo flew down to investigate. There was a young bat named Lysandro who hadn't mastered flying yet. His tiny wings fluttered aimlessly.

Aetharwoo didn't mind sharing night-time wonders. He swooped down and landed beside Lysandro.

"Lights like that must be magic," Aetharwoo said kindly.

"Lots of magic," Lysandro piped up. "Mom sends me up here to find the
